phases.name: Pre-Engagement Interactions
phases.description: Defines the scope, objectives, and rules of engagement for the penetration test. Establishes legal boundaries and communication channels.
objectives: Define scope of work and authorized targets
Establish rules of engagement (RoE)
Define communication channels and escalation procedures
Establish legal authorization (signed contracts/NDA)
Agree on timelines and reporting requirements
Define engagement type (blackbox/greybox/whitebox)
activities: Threat modeling and intelligence gathering goals

phases.name: Intelligence Gathering
phases.description: Comprehensive information gathering about the target organization using both passive and active reconnaissance techniques.
objectives: Map the target's digital footprint
Identify external-facing assets and technologies
Gather employee information for social engineering
Identify potential entry points
activities: OSINT: social media, job postings, public records

phases.name: Threat Modeling
phases.description: Analyze gathered intelligence to identify the most likely attack vectors and prioritize testing efforts based on business risk.
objectives: Identify critical assets and their attack surface
Prioritize vulnerabilities by business impact
Develop attack scenarios and hypotheses

phases.name: Vulnerability Analysis
phases.description: Systematic identification of vulnerabilities in the target environment using automated scanning and manual testing.
objectives: Identify security vulnerabilities across all in-scope systems
Validate scanner findings to eliminate false positives
Map vulnerabilities to CVE/CWE databases
activities: Manual testing to validate automated findings
CVE research for identified software versions
deliverables: Validated vulnerability list with risk ratings

phases.name: Exploitation
phases.description: Attempt to exploit identified vulnerabilities to demonstrate real-world impact and gain unauthorized access.
objectives: Confirm exploitability of identified vulnerabilities
Demonstrate business impact of successful exploitation
Gain initial foothold in the target environment
activities: Exploit known CVEs using Metasploit or custom exploits

phases.name: Post-Exploitation
phases.description: After gaining initial access, assess the extent of the compromise, maintain access, and demonstrate lateral movement capabilities.
objectives: Determine the extent of a real attacker's reach post-compromise
Demonstrate lateral movement and privilege escalation
Assess data exfiltration potential

phases.name: Reporting
phases.description: Document all findings in a professional report that provides actionable remediation guidance for both technical and executive audiences.
objectives: Communicate findings clearly to both technical and executive audiences
Provide actionable remediation guidance prioritized by risk
Document methodology and testing evidence
